a_image.alt: Webcore style mood board — Chaotic Early-Internet Nostalgia

sections.a_eyebrow: Retro · Early Web · Nostalgic · Bold Colors · Scattered
sections.a_description: Webcore is a self-aware celebration of the chaotic early internet — the era of tiled starfield backgrounds, auto-playing MIDIs, visitor counters, and 'under construction' GIF banners. It embraces the clashing neon colors, pixel fonts, and unpolished DIY energy of GeoCities and Angelfire personal pages, treating the visual chaos as an intentional aesthetic rather than amateur design.

Used in nostalgic digital art projects, zine-style personal sites, ironic web revival communities, indie game interfaces, and any creative context that celebrates the raw, unfiltered energy of the 1990s and early 2000s web before corporate design homogenized everything.

Design a card in the Webcore aesthetic — the chaotic, self-aware early-internet style inspired by GeoCities, Angelfire, and late-90s personal homepages. Use a deep navy-black starfield background (#0a0a2e) with bright, clashing accent colors: electric yellow (#ffff00), hot magenta (#ff00ff), cyber lime (#00ff00), and hyperlink blue (#0000ff). A pseudo-element creates a repeating star-dot pattern. Typography: Use Comic Neue (a refined Comic Sans successor) as the primary typeface — it captures that amateur homepage energy while remaining legible. Pair with Press Start 2P for the eyebrow label — a pixel font that channels 8-bit gaming culture and early web badge aesthetics. Root container: background #0a0a2e, color #00ff00, border-radius 0px (sharp — the early web didn't do rounded corners), 3px dashed border in #ff00ff. Inner container: padding 32px. Eyebrow: #ffff00 text, 10px Press Start 2P, uppercase, 3px letter-spacing, with a subtle text-shadow in #ff00ff. H3: #ff00ff, 26px Comic Neue bold, line-height 1.3, letter-spacing -1px. Body: #00ff00, 15px Comic Neue, line-height 1.7. Buttons: primary — background #ffff00, color #0a0a2e, 14px Press Start 2P, 2px solid #ff00ff border, 0px border-radius; hover: background #ff00ff, color #ffff00. Secondary — transparent, #00ff00, 2px dashed #00ff00 border; hover: background #00ff00, color #0a0a2e. Alternatives: VT323 for full monospace terminal vibes, Silkscreen for another pixel font.
{What this palette represents — the core identity}
